title: Fix enum variables in constraint solver producing invalid enum values (#7058)
body:
## Summary
When enum variables (rand or randc) are referenced in user constraints, the
constraint solver declares them as unconstrained bitvectors, which can produce
values outside the valid enum range. This adds implicit membership constraints
to restrict solver output to valid enum members (IEEE 1800-2017 §18.4.2).

## Issue
Fixes #7058 

## Changes
- `V3Randomize.cpp`: In `visit(AstClass*)`, after `clearConstraints()` and
  before constraint setup tasks, iterate over member variables. For each
  enum-typed variable with `user3()` (in the solver path), emit an implicit
  `hard()` constraint that restricts the SMT variable to valid enum values:
  `(__Vbv (or (= name (_ bv0 W)) (= name (_ bv1 W)) ...))`

## Test
- Added `test_regress/t/t_randc_enum_constraint.{v,py}` — verifies randc enum
  with user constraints only produces valid enum members

---
Written by Claude Code and PlanV GmbH.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
